title: Heliotropium gardneri
taxonomy: Heliotropium gardneri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to South America. The genus Heliotropium comprises over 240 species, and Heliotropium gardneri is one of them. This plant is also known by its synonym Tournefortia gardneri. Further classification at the infraspecific level is not available.

intro: Heliotropium gardneri, also known as Argentinian heliotrope, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, expectorant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Heliotropium gardneri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activities. In herbal medicine, Heliotropium gardneri is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma.
